attacks.name: Dread End
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Darkness Pokémon in play.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Power Accelerator
attacks.effect: You may attach a Darkness Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dynamax Cannon
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon VMAX, this attack does 120 more damage.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Revenge Blast
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Max Transform
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Stick On
attacks.effect: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: G-Max Drill
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 more damage for each extra Darkness Energy attached to this Pokémon (in addition to this attack's cost). You can't add more than 100 damage in this way.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Spiky Knuckle
attacks.effect: Put 2 Darkness Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Giga Impact
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: G-Max Riot
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 80 more damage.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Electric Riot
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 90 more damage.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: G-Max Pump
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Body Surf
attacks.effect: Attach a Water Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon. If you do, switch it with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Ocean Loop
attacks.effect: Put 2 Water Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: G-Max Centiferno
attacks.effect: This attack does 40 more damage for each Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you did any damage with this attack, you may attach a Fire Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Radiating Heat
attacks.effect: You may discard an Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: G-Max Wildfire
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Max Beating
attacks.effect: You may discard up to 3 Grass Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 50 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Forest Feast
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Grass Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Wood Hammer
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Cotton Guard
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Double-Edge
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its majestic horns are meant only to impress the opposite gender. They never see use in battle.

attacks.name: Expand
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 10 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: If its fleece grows too long, Wooloo won't be able to move. Cloth made with the wool of this Pokémon is surprisingly strong.

attacks.name: Pluck
attacks.effect: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Smart enough to use tools in battle, these Pokémon have been seen picking up rocks and flinging them or using ropes to wrap up enemies.
It will bravely challenge any opponent, no matter how powerful. This Pokémon benefits from every battle—even a defeat increases its strength a bit.
It stashes berries in its tail—so many berries that they fall out constantly. But this Pokémon is a bit slow-witted, so it doesn't notice the loss.
Found throughout the Galar region, this Pokémon becomes uneasy if its cheeks are ever completely empty of berries.
It knows the forest inside and out. If it comes across a wounded Pokémon, Oranguru will gather medicinal herbs to treat it.

attacks.name: Mad Party
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile that has the Mad Party attack.

flavorText: It excels at digging holes. Using its ears, it can dig a nest 33 feet deep in one night.

attacks.name: Feather Slice
attacks.effect: You may discard a card from your hand. If you do, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: Swanna start to dance at dusk. The one dancing in the middle is the leader of the flock.
They are better at swimming than flying, and they happily eat their favorite food, peat moss, as they dive underwater.

attacks.name: Energy Assist
attacks.effect: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Its body secretes oil that this Pokémon spreads over its nest as a coating to protect it from dust. Cinccino won't tolerate even a speck of the stuff.

attacks.name: Glance
attacks.effect: Look at the top card of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Tail Slap
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: The way it brushes away grime with its tail can be helpful when cleaning. But its focus on spotlessness can make cleaning more of a hassle.

attacks.name: Metal Sharpener
attacks.effect: Attach a Metal Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: Its body resembles polished metal, and it's both lightweight and strong. The only drawback is that it rusts easily.

attacks.name: Vengeful Stomp
attacks.effect: If your Benched Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: They came over from another region long ago and worked together with humans. Their green skin is resistant to water.
It digs up the ground with its trunk. It's also very strong, being able to carry loads of over five tons without any problem at all.

attacks.name: Iron Wings
attacks.effect: You may discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, during your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 100 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: This Pokémon reigns supreme in the skies of the Galar region. The black luster of its steel body could drive terror into the heart of any foe.

attacks.name: Damage Rush
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Living in mud with a high iron content has given it a strong steel body.
What appears to be an iron helmet is actually hardened hair. This Pokémon lives for the thrill of battle.
Living with a savage, seafaring people has toughened this Pokémon's body so much that parts of it have turned to iron.

attacks.name: Energy Press
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: With the hair wrapped around its body helping to enhance its muscles, this Pokémon can overwhelm even Machamp.

attacks.name: False Surrender
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: When it gets down on all fours as if to beg for forgiveness, it's trying to lure opponents in so that it can stab them with its spear-like hair.
Through its nose, it sucks in the emanations produced by people and Pokémon when they feel annoyed. It thrives off this negative energy.

attacks.name: Thief
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals their hand. Choose a card you find there and put it on the bottom of their deck.

flavorText: It secretly marks potential targets with a scent. By following the scent, it stalks its targets and steals from them when they least expect it.

attacks.name: Instigate
attacks.effect: Your opponent shuffles their hand and puts it on the bottom of their deck. If they put any cards on the bottom of their deck in this way, they draw 3 cards.

flavorText: Aided by the soft pads on its feet, it silently raids the food stores of other Pokémon. It survives off its ill-gotten gains.

attacks.name: Obstruct
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon.

flavorText: It evolved after experiencing numerous fights. While crossing its arms, it lets out a shout that would make any opponent flinch.

attacks.name: Night Slash
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This very aggressive Pokémon will recklessly challenge opponents stronger than itself.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Thought to be the oldest form of Zigzagoon, it moves in zigzags and wreaks havoc upon its surroundings.

attacks.name: Severe Poison
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 4 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon during Pokémon Checkup.

flavorText: This Pokémon consumes particles that contaminate the air. Instead of leaving droppings, it expels clean air.

attacks.name: Ascension
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Its body is full of poisonous gas. It floats into garbage dumps, seeking out the fumes of raw, rotting trash.

attacks.name: Power Press
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has at least 1 extra Energy attached (in addition to this attack's cost), this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: It stands in grasslands, watching the sun's descent from zenith to horizon. This Pokémon has a talent for delivering dynamic kicks.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Team Attack
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has "Falinks" in its name.

flavorText: Five of them are troopers, and one is the brass. The brass's orders are absolute.

attacks.name: Octolock
attacks.effect: Until this Grapploct leaves the Active Spot, the Defending Pokémon's attacks cost ColorlessColorless more, and the Defending Pokémon can't retreat. This effect can't be applied more than once.

attacks.name: Tough Swing
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: A body made up of nothing but muscle makes the grappling moves this Pokémon performs with its tentacles tremendously powerful.

attacks.name: Bind
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It's very curious, but its means of investigating things is to try to punch them with its tentacles. The search for food is what brings it onto land.

attacks.name: Coil
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's attacks do 120 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its unique style of coiling allows it to blast sand out of its sand sac more efficiently.

attacks.name: Sand Attack
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

flavorText: It spews sand from its nostrils. While the enemy is blinded, it burrows into the ground to hide.
It's usually peaceful, but the vandalism of mines enrages it. Offenders will be incinerated with flames that reach 2,700 degrees Fahrenheit.
It forms coal inside its body. Coal dropped by this Pokémon once helped fuel the lives of people in the Galar region.
Most of its body has the same composition as coal. Fittingly, this Pokémon was first discovered in coal mines about 400 years ago.

attacks.name: Spreading Spite
attacks.effect: For each damage counter on this Galarian Runerigus, put 2 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Mad Hammer
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: A powerful curse was woven into an ancient painting. After absorbing the spirit of a Yamask, the painting began to move.

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: A clay slab with cursed engravings took possession of a Yamask. The slab is said to be absorbing the Yamask's dark power.

attacks.name: Meteor Assault
attacks.effect: This Pokémon can't use Meteor Assault again until it leaves the Active Spot.

flavorText: Only Farfetch'd that have survived many battles can attain this evolution. When this Pokémon's leek withers, it will retire from combat.

attacks.name: Rock Smash
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: The Farfetch'd of the Galar region are brave warriors, and they wield thick, tough leeks in battle.

attacks.name: Phantom Force
attacks.effect: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: When it isn't battling, it keeps Dreepy in the holes on its horns. Once a fight starts, it launches the Dreepy like supersonic missiles.

attacks.name: Summon
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Dreepy and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It's capable of flying faster than 120 mph. It battles alongside Dreepy and dotes on them until they successfully evolve.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: After being reborn as a ghost Pokémon, Dreepy wanders the areas it used to inhabit back when it was alive in prehistoric seas.

attacks.name: Replenish Time
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from each of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: These intelligent Pokémon touch horns with each other to share information between them.

attacks.name: Decorate
attacks.effect: Attach any number of basic Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Draining Kiss
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When it trusts a Trainer, it will treat them to berries it's decorated with cream.

attacks.name: Aromatherapy
attacks.effect: Heal 10 damage from each of your Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon was born from sweet-smelling particles in the air. Its body is made of cream.

attacks.name: Dripping Grudge
attacks.effect: Put 1 damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon for each Pokémon in your discard pile.

flavorText: It emits psychic power strong enough to cause headaches as a deterrent to the approach of others.

attacks.name: Calm Mind
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Brutal Swing
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: No matter who you are, if you bring strong emotions near this Pokémon, it will silence you violently.

attacks.name: Find a Friend
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Via the protrusion on its head, it senses other creatures' emotions. If you don't have a calm disposition, it will never warm up to you.

attacks.name: Mad Party
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile that has the Mad Party attack.

flavorText: This species lives in antique teapots. Most pots are forgeries, but on rare occasions, an authentic work is found.

attacks.name: Furtive Drop
attacks.effect: Put 2 damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon is said to have been born when a lonely spirit possessed a cold, leftover cup of tea.

attacks.name: Mad Party
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile that has the Mad Party attack.

flavorText: Its upper whiskers are sensors that survey its surroundings. Its lower whiskers are organs that shoot electricity.

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: Its shell is overflowing with its heightened otherworldly energy. The ectoplasm serves as protection for this Pokémon's core spirit.

attacks.name: Cursed Drop
attacks.effect: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: Watch your step when wandering areas oceans once covered. What looks like a stone could be this Pokémon, and it will curse you if you kick it.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Brave and prideful, this Pokémon dashes airily through the forest, its steps aided by the psychic power stored in the fur on its fetlocks.

attacks.name: Psy Bolt
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: This Pokémon will look into your eyes and read the contents of your heart. If it finds evil there, it promptly hides away.
The shaking of its freezing upper half is what generates its electricity. It has a hard time walking around.

attacks.name: Amping Up
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Amping Up attack does 90 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Giga Impact
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: In ancient times, it was unbeatable thanks to its powerful lower body, but it went extinct anyway after it depleted all its plant-based food sources.

attacks.name: Torment
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't use that attack.

attacks.name: Spark
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: As it eats the seeds stored up in its pocket-like pouches, this Pokémon is not just satisfying its constant hunger. It's also generating electricity.

attacks.name: Shocking Needles
attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads. If at least 2 of them are heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It feeds on seaweed, using its teeth to scrape it off rocks. Electric current flows from the tips of its spines.

attacks.name: Risk Taker
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard the top 5 cards of your opponent's deck. If tails, discard the top 5 cards of your deck.

attacks.name: Thunder Jolt
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: When this Pokémon sounds as if it's strumming a guitar, it's actually clawing at the protrusions on its chest to generate electricity.
It stores poison in an internal poison sac and secretes that poison through its skin. If you touch this Pokémon, a tingling sensation follows.

attacks.name: Big Bite
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Fighting Fangs
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V or Pokémon-GX, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: This Pokémon generates electricity and channels it into its legs to keep them going strong. Boltund can run nonstop for three full days.

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.effect: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon is very popular as a herding dog in the Galar region. As it runs, it generates electricity from the base of its tail.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: One boy's invention led to the development of many different machines that take advantage of Rotom's unique capabilities.

attacks.name: Hard Face
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 60 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Cold Breath
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Though it's able to capture prey by freezing its surroundings, it has trouble eating the prey afterward because its mouth is on top of its head.
Powerful legs and jaws made it the apex predator of its time. Its own overhunting of its prey was what drove it to extinction.

attacks.name: Blizzard
attacks.effect: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It drifted in on the flow of ocean waters from a frigid place. It keeps its head iced constantly to make sure it stays nice and cold.
It shows no mercy to any who desecrate fields and mountains. It will fly around on its icy wings, causing a blizzard to chase offenders away.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It spits out thread imbued with a frigid sort of energy and uses it to tie its body to branches, disguising itself as an icicle while it sleeps.

attacks.name: Spiral Jet
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Water Energy cards from your hand. If you don't, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: This Pokémon has a jaw that's as sharp as a spear and as strong as steel. Apparently Barraskewda's flesh is surprisingly tasty, too.
If it sees any movement around it, this Pokémon charges for it straightaway, leading with its sharply pointed jaw. It's very proud of that jaw.

attacks.name: Dive
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It's so strong that it can knock out some opponents in a single hit, but it also may forget what it's battling midfight.

attacks.name: Vise Bite
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Jaw Lock
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: This Pokémon rapidly extends its retractable neck to sink its sharp fangs into distant enemies and take them down.
It starts off battles by attacking with its rock-hard horn, but as soon as the opponent flinches, this Pokémon bites down and never lets go.

attacks.name: Aqua Bullet
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It has many hidden capabilities, such as fingertips that can shoot water and a membrane on its back that it can use to glide through the air.
A clever combatant, this Pokémon battles using water balloons created with moisture secreted from its palms.
When scared, this Pokémon cries. Its tears pack the chemical punch of 100 onions, and attackers won't be able to resist weeping.

attacks.name: Blizzard
attacks.effect: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Crushing Headbutt
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Crushing Headbutt.

flavorText: On days when blizzards blow through, it comes down to where people live. It stashes food in the snowball on its head, taking it home for later.

attacks.name: Ice Punch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It lived in snowy areas for so long that its fire sac cooled off and atrophied. It now has an organ that generates cold instead.

attacks.name: Aurora Loop
attacks.effect: Put 2 Water Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.

flavorText: Said to be the embodiment of north winds, it can instantly purify filthy, murky water.

fullDisplayName: Galarian Mr. Rime SV021/SV122 Shining Fates

attacks.name: Mad Party
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile that has the Mad Party attack.

flavorText: It's highly skilled at tap-dancing. It waves its cane of ice in time with its graceful movements.

fullDisplayName: Galarian Mr. Mime SV020/SV122 Shining Fates

attacks.name: Reflect
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its talent is tap-dancing. It can also manipulate temperatures to create a floor of ice, which this Pokémon can kick up to use as a barrier.

attacks.name: Hundred Foot Flames
attacks.effect: For each Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon, discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: When it heats up, its body temperature reaches about 1,500 degrees Fahrenheit. It lashes its body like a whip and launches itself at enemies.
It stores flammable gas in its body and uses it to generate heat. The yellow sections on its belly get particularly hot.

attacks.name: Flare Striker
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It's skilled at both offense and defense, and it gets pumped up when cheered on. But if it starts showboating, it could put itself in a tough spot.

attacks.name: Flame Charge
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Fire Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It kicks berries right off the branches of trees and then juggles them with its feet, practicing its footwork.

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It has special pads on the backs of its feet, and one on its nose. Once it's raring to fight, these pads radiate tremendous heat.
Eating a sweet apple caused its evolution. A nectarous scent wafts from its body, luring in the bug Pokémon it preys on.

attacks.name: Acid Spray
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It ate a sour apple, and that induced its evolution. In its cheeks, it stores an acid capable of causing chemical burns.

attacks.name: Withdraw
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks.

flavorText: It spends its entire life inside an apple. It hides from its natural enemies, bird Pokémon, by pretending it's just an apple and nothing more.

attacks.name: Blessing of Fluff
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 Grass Energy cards and attach them to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: The cotton on the head of this Pokémon can be spun into a glossy, gorgeous yarn—a Galar regional specialty.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It whirls around in the wind while singing a joyous song. This delightful display has charmed many into raising this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Brainwave
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Psychic Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It emits psychic energy to observe and study what's around it—and what's around it can include things over six miles away.

attacks.name: Reflect
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 40 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: As it grows inside its shell, it uses its psychic abilities to monitor the outside world and prepare for evolution.
Often found in gardens, this Pokémon has hairs on its body that it uses to assess its surroundings.
By drumming, it taps into the power of its special tree stump. The roots of the stump follow its direction in battle.
The faster a Thwackey can beat out a rhythm with its two sticks, the more respect it wins from its peers.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: When it uses its special stick to strike up a beat, the sound waves produced carry revitalizing energy to the plants and flowers in the area.

attacks.name: Splitting Arrow
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: As if wielding a bow, it launches the arrow quills hidden among the feathers of its wings. Decidueye's shots never miss.
It throws one knifelike feather after another at its enemies, and each one precisely strikes a weak point. These feathers are known as blade quills.

attacks.name: Wind Shard
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: At a distance, it launches its sharp feathers while flying about. If the enemy gets too close, Rowlet switches tactics and delivers vicious kicks.

attacks.name: Adornment
attacks.effect: For each of your Benched Pokémon, search your deck for a Psychic Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: G-Max Whisk
attacks.effect: Discard any amount of Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Search your deck for a Trainer card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Attach up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon VMAX. If you attached any Energy cards in this way, discard your hand.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Search your deck for an Energy card and a Darkness Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Draw 2 cards. If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn, draw 2 more cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Search your deck for up to 2 Rare Fossil cards and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, draw 3 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Search your deck for up to 3 different Item cards that have the word "Ball" in their name, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

attacks.name: Sugary Sprinkles
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from each of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sweet Splash
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Heal 50 damage from both Active Pokémon.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

The attacks of the Zacian V this card is attached to do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

The Zamazenta V this card is attached to gets +70 HP.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Draw 2 cards. If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn, draw 2 more cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Search your deck for up to 3 different Item cards that have the word "Ball" in their name, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

attacks.name: Helping Hand
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It uses the horns on its head to sense the emotions of others. Males will act as valets for those they serve, looking after their every need.

attacks.name: Max Jet
attacks.effect: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Beak Catch
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Spit Shot
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 160 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Stumbling Press
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Signs of Evolution
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a card that evolves from Eevee, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Wild Kick
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It has the ability to alter the composition of its body to suit its surrounding environment.

attacks.name: Max Transform
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Stick On
attacks.effect: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: It digs up the ground with its trunk. It's also very strong, being able to carry loads of over five tons without any problem at all.

attacks.name: Call for Goons
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: It secretly marks potential targets with a scent. By following the scent, it stalks its targets and steals from them when they least expect it.

attacks.name: Instigate
attacks.effect: Your opponent shuffles their hand and puts it on the bottom of their deck. If they put any cards on the bottom of their deck in this way, they draw 3 cards.

flavorText: Aided by the soft pads on its feet, it silently raids the food stores of other Pokémon. It survives off its ill-gotten gains.

attacks.name: Amazing Destruction
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is Knocked Out.

flavorText: When its life comes to an end, it absorbs the life energy of every living thing and turns into a cocoon once more.

attacks.name: Stealth Poison
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Venomous Fang
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: Although the poison from its fangs isn't that strong, it's potent enough to weaken prey that gets caught in its web.

attacks.name: Severe Poison
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 4 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon during Pokémon Checkup.

flavorText: This Pokémon consumes particles that contaminate the air. Instead of leaving droppings, it expels clean air.

attacks.name: Ascension
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Its body is full of poisonous gas. It floats into garbage dumps, seeking out the fumes of raw, rotting trash.

attacks.name: Land's Pulse
attacks.effect: If a Stadium is in play, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: Its nest is a sloped, bowl-like pit in the desert. Once something has fallen in, there is no escape.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Max Discharge
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Spark
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Electro Wheel
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, switch it with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Famished
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: As it eats the seeds stored up in its pocket-like pouches, this Pokémon is not just satisfying its constant hunger. It's also generating electricity.

attacks.name: Famished
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: As it eats the seeds stored up in its pocket-like pouches, this Pokémon is not just satisfying its constant hunger. It's also generating electricity.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: One boy's invention led to the development of many different machines that take advantage of Rotom's unique capabilities.

attacks.name: Raid
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon evolved from Luxio during this turn, this attack does 100 more damage.

flavorText: Luxray's ability to see through objects comes in handy when it's scouting for danger.
Strong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws. A light scratch causes fainting in foes.

attacks.name: Under Pressure
attacks.effect: If your opponent has 3 or fewer Prize cards remaining, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: All of its fur dazzles if danger is sensed. It flees while the foe is momentarily blinded.
It shows no mercy to any who desecrate fields and mountains. It will fly around on its icy wings, causing a blizzard to chase offenders away.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It spits out thread imbued with a frigid sort of energy and uses it to tie its body to branches, disguising itself as an icicle while it sleeps.

attacks.name: Spit-Out Shot
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: This hungry Pokémon swallows Arrokuda whole. Occasionally, it makes a mistake and tries to swallow a Pokémon other than its preferred prey.
This Pokémon rapidly extends its retractable neck to sink its sharp fangs into distant enemies and take them down.
It starts off battles by attacking with its rock-hard horn, but as soon as the opponent flinches, this Pokémon bites down and never lets go.

attacks.name: Dynamite Steam
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 120 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It expels its internal steam from the arms on its back. It has enough power to blow away a mountain.
It starts its life with a wondrous power that permits it to bond with any kind of Pokémon.
Its flotation sac developed as a result of pursuing aquatic prey. It can double as a rubber raft.
It inflates the flotation sac around its neck and pokes its head out of the water to see what is going on.

attacks.name: Amazing Surge
attacks.effect: This attack does 80 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It is said to have widened the seas by causing downpours. It had been asleep in a marine trench.
It makes its nest in the shade of corals. If it senses danger, it spits murky ink and flees.

attacks.name: Counter
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon was damaged by an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does that much more damage.

attacks.name: Max Pyro Ball
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.
V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Amazing Blaze
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 60 damage to itself.

flavorText: When Reshiram's tail flares, the heat energy moves the atmosphere and changes the world's weather.

attacks.name: Suctioning Vines
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Once the vines on Zarude's body tear off, they become nutrients in the soil. This helps the plants of the forest grow.

attacks.name: Enriching Seeds
attacks.effect: Heal all damage from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: The cotton on the head of this Pokémon can be spun into a glossy, gorgeous yarn—a Galar regional specialty.
It whirls around in the wind while singing a joyous song. This delightful display has charmed many into raising this Pokémon.
By drumming, it taps into the power of its special tree stump. The roots of the stump follow its direction in battle.
The faster a Thwackey can beat out a rhythm with its two sticks, the more respect it wins from its peers.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: When it uses its special stick to strike up a beat, the sound waves produced carry revitalizing energy to the plants and flowers in the area.

attacks.name: Swinging Chain
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each Grass Energy attached to this Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Max Anchor
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Max Anchor.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Anchor Anger
attacks.effect: If any of your Grass Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack during their last turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Giga Hammer
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Giga Hammer.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Splitting Arrow
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: As if wielding a bow, it launches the arrow quills hidden among the feathers of its wings. Decidueye's shots never miss.
It throws one knifelike feather after another at its enemies, and each one precisely strikes a weak point. These feathers are known as blade quills.

attacks.name: Wind Shard
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: At a distance, it launches its sharp feathers while flying about. If the enemy gets too close, Rowlet switches tactics and delivers vicious kicks.

attacks.name: Attach Leaves
attacks.effect: Attach up to 2 Grass Energy cards from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: Bunches of delicious fruit grow around its neck. In warm areas, many ranches raise Tropius.
It prefers harsh environments such as deserts. It can survive for 30 days on water stored in its body.
It has the power to travel across time, but it is said to appear only in peaceful times.

attacks.name: Nosedive
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: This six-legged Pokémon is easily capable of transporting an adult in flight. The wings on its tail help it stay balanced.
If it flaps its wings really fast, it can generate shock waves that will shatter windows in the area.

attacks.name: Adornment
attacks.effect: For each of your Benched Pokémon, search your deck for a Psychic Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: G-Max Whisk
attacks.effect: Discard any amount of Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Wind Shard
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: At a distance, it launches its sharp feathers while flying about. If the enemy gets too close, Rowlet switches tactics and delivers vicious kicks.

attacks.name: Attach Leaves
attacks.effect: Attach up to 2 Grass Energy cards from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: Bunches of delicious fruit grow around its neck. In warm areas, many ranches raise Tropius.
It prefers harsh environments such as deserts. It can survive for 30 days on water stored in its body.
It has the power to travel across time, but it is said to appear only in peaceful times.

attacks.name: Nosedive
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: This six-legged Pokémon is easily capable of transporting an adult in flight. The wings on its tail help it stay balanced.
If it flaps its wings really fast, it can generate shock waves that will shatter windows in the area.
